title: Scroll needed on saved filter views
excerpt: Please add a vertical scroll bar to the filter dropdown arrow on boards. I have a Saved View on the “Filter by Anything” button on a large Team board - on the Advanced View I have more than 15 individual filter lines whi…

posts.author: VeronicaK (Veronica Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add a vertical scroll bar to the filter dropdown arrow on boards. I have a Saved View on the “Filter by Anything” button on a large Team board - on the Advanced View I have more than 15 individual filter lines which I change regularly as groups get added and removed. I can’t see any below about the 10th line as if I scroll the whole board scrolls but the filter dropdown doesn’t move. The only way I can update the lower lines is by zooming out the entire browser window and then the font is almost impossible to see!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @VeronicaK,

Is it possible for you to send over a short demonstration of this issue? You’re welcome to send this over a private message via the forum. You can use the online, free tool, loom.com to do so. I’d love to share this internally as soon as possible incase this is a bug in the system

posts.author: VeronicaK (Veronica Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BiancaT, I can’t use Loom over our work network. But I think a screenprint may explain it - see attached - I’ve circled the relevant bits in red.

I created a test board which has 18 groups. I then created an advanced filter and selected 14 of the groups.

If I need to change those filters or add another one, I can’t see past the 13th line. The board will scroll with that filter dropdown open, but not the filter dropdown itself.

Let me know if this isn’t clear - thank you.

filter-view1191×842 102 KB

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks so much for sharing! I will follow up internally about this for you. In the meantime, can you please let me know if this same behaviour occurs in an incognito window (i.e. private browser)?

posts.author: VeronicaK (Veronica Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bianca, yes I tried it in an incognito window (using Edge browser which is our default work network browser) - same thing happens.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for letting me know! I have chased our product team on this, however in the meantime, can you please send me a private message (via community) with your email? If this is a bug, we can get this looked into via our technical support team as soon as possible!

posts.author: VeronicaK (Veronica Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml: Silly question, but do I just reply to you here to send a private message?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Sent you a message! You should see a notification pop up in the top right hand corner

posts.author: lpenndorf (Lori)
posts.cookedHtml: this issue still seems to exist. i have just created a rather lengthy filter to handle a special situation. i need well over 20 filters in place to get what we need from another tool’s data we are importing. i cannot scroll to add all the filters. I am using Chrome on a windows computer. i’ve tried Edge and Firefox as well. The only way I can see the bottom of the filters is to shrink my browser window down to about 50% and even on my 23" monitor I can barely read the screen that way, so that’s not workable. Hoping there’s a workaround for this situation that you found from working with Veronica last year.

posts.author: sgprf (sg)
posts.cookedHtml: This is something I wish for every day. In order to update some of my more complex views, I have to zoom out to the point where the text is nearly illegible or stretch out my window to be 2x the height of my display (or do both).

posts.author: sgprf (sg)
posts.cookedHtml: Following up to say it’s scrollable now!

posts.author: VeronicaK (Veronica Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml: That’s great! Thanks for the alert post sg, so good to know this is fixed
